› You are the worker in a disposable local telemetry drill for codex-terminal-
  manager.

  Do not edit files. Do not commit. Inspect the repository only enough to
  produce a concise receipt.

  Task:
  - Confirm the local telemetry workflow commands that should be used after
  this drill.
  - Mention the expected export artifacts: telemetry-events.json, telemetry-
  summary.json, telemetry-report.md.
  - End with a short "Worker receipt" section and say whether you changed any
  files.


⚠ Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex
  can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused
  skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

• I’ll inspect the repo commands without touching files, then return a short
  receipt with the telemetry workflow and artifact names.

• The repo has a dedicated docs/local-telemetry-workflow.md, and the CLI/export

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Confirmed from docs/local-telemetry-workflow.md, README.md, and workerctl/
  export.py.

  Use these local telemetry workflow commands after the drill:

  scripts/workerctl telemetry --summary --run <run_id>
  scripts/workerctl telemetry --run <run_id>
  scripts/workerctl telemetry --search manager --run <run_id>
  scripts/workerctl export-task <task> --zip --include-transcripts
  scripts/workerctl sessions --state active
  scripts/workerctl reconcile --stale-cycles-seconds 1

  For durable JSON evidence, use:

  scripts/workerctl telemetry --summary --run <run_id> --json > telemetry-
  summary.json
  scripts/workerctl telemetry --run <run_id> --json > telemetry-events.json

  Expected export artifacts are included by export-task by default:

  - telemetry-events.json
  - telemetry-summary.json
  - telemetry-report.md

  Worker Receipt

  Inspected only: docs/local-telemetry-workflow.md, README.md, workerctl/
  export.py, and git status --short.

  Changed files: no. I did not edit or commit anything. Current status showed an
  existing untracked directory: docs/live-qa-artifacts/2026-05-20-local-
  telemetry-drill/.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


› Please propose 2-4 acceptance criteria for the current slice. Split them into
  must-have current-task criteria and follow-up criteria. Include the
  verification you expect for each.


• Must-have current-task criteria:

  1. docs/local-telemetry-workflow.md remains the canonical workflow reference.
     Verification: inspect the doc and confirm it lists telemetry --summary,
     timeline, --search manager, export-task --zip --include-transcripts,
     sessions --state active, and reconcile --stale-cycles-seconds 1.
  2. export-task includes the three telemetry artifacts by default.
     Verification: inspect workerctl/export.py or run a disposable export and
     confirm telemetry-events.json, telemetry-summary.json, and telemetry-
     report.md are written and listed in manifest.json.
  3. The drill receipt is inspection-only.
     Verification: run git status --short and confirm no files were changed by
     the worker; any pre-existing untracked drill artifact directory is
     explicitly called out.

  Follow-up criteria:

  1. Add a lightweight automated smoke check for telemetry export contents.
     Verification: run the smoke script/test and confirm it creates an export
     bundle containing the three telemetry artifacts plus manifest entries for
     each.

  summary/events, saved command artifacts, export manifest, and telemetry report
